question: Salinity of freshwater sources like stream, lake and river is less than ........[2022]
question_latex: Salinity of freshwater sources like stream, lake and river is less than ........[2022]
solution: Salinity of freshwater sources like stream, lake and river is less than 5 ppt (parts per thousand).

question: Reproductive isolation between segments of a single population is termed as
question_latex: Reproductive isolation between segments of a single population is termed as
solution: Sympatry is the condition when selection may produce ecotypes living in adjacent habitats in the same geographic area and gives rise to sympatric speciation, i.e., formation of species within a single population by reproductive isolation or without geographical isolation.

question: Which of the following is INCORRECT with respect to niche. *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Which of the following is INCORRECT with respect to niche. *[MHT-CET 2024]
options: A. A unique niche helps in reducing competition for resources among species.
B. It can be shared by two different species.
C. It is specific to each species.
D. It deals with flow of energy.
answer: B. It can be shared by two different species.
solution: The niche is specific to each species, which means no two species can share the same niche.

question: The highest level of ecological hierarchy in ecological organization is ____ *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: The highest level of ecological hierarchy in ecological organization is \underline{\hspace{2cm}} *[MHT-CET 2024]
solution: The highest level of ecological hierarchy in ecological organization is Biome which constitutes a large regional terrestrial unit delimited by a specific climatic zone having major vegetation zone (plant communities) and the associated fauna.

question: The eggs of parasitic birds resemble the host bird's eggs in color and size. This is observed in ______ parasitism. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: The eggs of parasitic birds resemble the host bird's eggs in color and size. This is observed in \underline{\hspace{2cm}} parasitism. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: The eggs of parasitic birds resemble the host bird's eggs in color and size. This is observed in brood parasitism. Eggs of parasitic bird Asian koel hatch before that of its host common Indian crow.

question: The characters such as pointed elongated snout, strong and stout forelimbs, well developed claws are observed in ______ adaptation. [MHT-CET 2015]
question_latex: The characters such as pointed elongated snout, strong and stout forelimbs, well developed claws are observed in \underline{\hspace{2cm}} adaptation. [MHT-CET 2015]
solution: Pointed elongated snout, strong and stout forelimbs, and well developed claws are characteristics of fossorial (burrowing) adaptation.

question: Many fresh water animals cannot live for long in seawater and vice versa mainly because of [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Many fresh water animals cannot live for long in seawater and vice versa mainly because of [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Many fresh water animals cannot live for long in seawater and vice versa mainly because of osmotic problems.

question: Humus is present in
question_latex: Humus is present in
solution: In soil profile, A-horizon is present under the litter zone and is called as top-soil. It is the zone of eluviations that contains a relatively high content of organic matter but mixed with mineral water. The $A_1$ region is dark and rich in organic matter. Finely divided organic matter here, becomes mixed with the mineral matter and is known as humus.

question: Given below is the age pyramid for Hungary. Which type of growth does it represent? [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Given below is the age pyramid for Hungary. Which type of growth does it represent? [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Negative growth will be seen when pre-reproductive population is less than post-reproductive population.

question: Ratio between mortality and natality is called
question_latex: Ratio between mortality and natality is called
solution: Vital index represents the ratio between natality (birth rate) and mortality (death rate). It determines the normal rate of growth of population and can be calculated by the formula: $\text{Vital index} = \frac{\text{Natality}}{\text{Mortality}} \times 100$

question: An individual has birth and death, similarly a population has ______ and ______ [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: An individual has birth and death, similarly a population has \underline{\hspace{2cm}} and \underline{\hspace{2cm}} [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: An individual has birth and death, similarly a population has natality and mortality.

question: Which among the following is the most ecologically relevant environmental factor? [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Which among the following is the most ecologically relevant environmental factor? [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Temperature is the most ecologically relevant environmental factor. Average temperature on land varies from subzero in polar to $59^\circ$ in tropical deserts in summer.

question: Under particular set of selection pressures, oysters and pelagic fishes show ..[2022]
question_latex: Under particular set of selection pressures, oysters and pelagic fishes show ..[2022]
options: A. small number of large sized offsprings.
B. large number of small sized offsprings.
C. small number of small sized offsprings.
D. large number of large sized offsprings.
answer: B. large number of small sized offsprings.
solution: Under particular set of selection pressures, oysters and pelagic fishes show large number of small sized offsprings.

question: Which of the following statement is wrong
question_latex: Which of the following statement is wrong
options: A. When resources in the habitat are unlimited, each species has the ability to realise fully its innate potential to grow in number
B. In 1981, the r value for human population in India was 0.0205
C. Any species growing exponentially under unlimited resource conditions can reach enormous population
D. The shape of exponential growth curve is S shaped
answer: D. The shape of exponential growth curve is S shaped
solution: The shape of exponential growth curve is J-shaped, not S-shaped. S-shaped curve is for logistic growth.

question: The percentage of soil volume occupied by pore space is called porosity of soil. It is minimum in
question_latex: The percentage of soil volume occupied by pore space is called porosity of soil. It is minimum in
solution: Porosity is 30% in sandy soil, 45% in loam and 50% in clay soil. Hence porosity is minimum in sandy soil.

question: Mean annual precipitation is maximum in ____ forest. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Mean annual precipitation is maximum in \underline{\hspace{2cm}} forest. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Mean annual precipitation is maximum in tropical forest with minimum temperature fluctuation.

question: A high density of tiger population in an area can result in
question_latex: A high density of tiger population in an area can result in
solution: A high density of tiger population in an area can result in intraspecific competition.

question: If population is showing a declining trend, it must be having more individuals in________ [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: If population is showing a declining trend, it must be having more individuals in \underline{\hspace{2cm}} [MHT-CET 2020]
options: D. Both pre-reproductive and reproductive age groups
solution: Population with post reproductive age group shows declining trend of population. Pre reproductive and reproductive age groups will show increasing population.

question: Which of the following pair of organisms are euryhaline in nature? *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Which of the following pair of organisms are euryhaline in nature? *[MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Clams and barnacles are euryhaline in nature as these organisms are tolerant for a wide range of salinities.

question: Pedology refers to study of
question_latex: Pedology refers to study of
solution: Pedology (GK. Pedon = soil; logos = study) is the study of soil in their natural environment. It deals with pedogenesis (formation of soil), soil morphology and soil classification.

question: Population density of terrestrial organisms is measured in terms of individuals per
question_latex: Population density of terrestrial organisms is measured in terms of individuals per
solution: Population density means the number of individuals of a species per unit area or volume. Space or area for terrestrial habitat is measured in two dimensions ($m^2$) while for aquatic habitats, it is measured in three dimensions ($m^3$).

question: Students of class XII were given a list of following characters such as clawed toes, curved beak, feathers, pneumatic bones, wings and fur. In this list, how many are volant adaptations? [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: Students of class XII were given a list of following characters such as clawed toes, curved beak, feathers, pneumatic bones, wings and fur. In this list, how many are volant adaptations? [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: Feathers, pneumatic bones and wings are three flight adaptations in the given list. Wait - the answer is marked (C) which is 5. But per the solution only 3 are listed. The answer key states C (5) but the solution mentions feathers, pneumatic bones and wings as three flight adaptations. There may be an error in the original; the answer as per the PDF is C.

question: The type of population, where pre-reproductive animals occur in large numbers, is
question_latex: The type of population, where pre-reproductive animals occur in large numbers, is
solution: In a growing population, the pre-reproductive, i.e., immature animals occur in large number.

question: Plants which behave as mesophytes in rainy season and as xerophytes in summers are called
question_latex: Plants which behave as mesophytes in rainy season and as xerophytes in summers are called
solution: Plants which behave as mesophytes in rainy season and as xerophytes in summers are called trophophytes.

question: The term 'ecology' was first used by A but B introduced the term. Choose the correct option. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: The term 'ecology' was first used by A but B introduced the term. Choose the correct option. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Term ecology was first used by Reiter but E. Haeckel gave substance to the term (introduced) ecology. So option A (Reiter - E. Haeckel) is correct.

question: Given below are two statements. Statement I: Under unlimited resources conditions every species is capable of growing exponentially. Statement II: A population growing in a habitat with limited resources initially shows a very slow rate of growth. In the light of above statement choose the correct option. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Given below are two statements. Statement I: Under unlimited resources conditions every species is capable of growing exponentially. Statement II: A population growing in a habitat with limited resources initially shows a very slow rate of growth. In the light of above statement choose the correct option. [MHT-CET 2024]
options: A. Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
B. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
C. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
D. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
answer: D. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
solution: Both statement I and statement II are correct with respect to population growth rate.

question: Select the pair of animal which lacks specific respiratory organs. [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: Select the pair of animal which lacks specific respiratory organs. [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: Jelly fishes and sponges show tissue level of organization; organ systems are absent so they do not have specific respiratory organ.

question: Population growth curve in most animals, except humans is [MHT CET 2004]
question_latex: Population growth curve in most animals, except humans is [MHT CET 2004]
solution: Population growth curve in most animals except humans is S-shaped, while in humans, it is J-shaped.

question: In colloquial language habitat is a postal address while niche is the ______ of the organism. *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: In colloquial language habitat is a postal address while niche is the \underline{\hspace{2cm}} of the organism. *[MHT-CET 2024]
solution: In colloquial language habitat is a postal address while niche is the Profession of the organism.

question: In animals fur and feathers are coated with________ [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: In animals fur and feathers are coated with \underline{\hspace{2cm}} [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: In animals fur and feather is exoskeleton giving protection; they are coated with wax to prevent entry of water and prevent from dehydration.

question: The size of the clay particle is less than
question_latex: The size of the clay particle is less than
solution: The size of clay particle is less than $0.002$ mm. The size of silt particle varies from $0.002$–$0.02$ mm.

question: Given below are two statements with respect to Niche of an organism. Statement-I: A niche is a concept, of how an organism lives or survives in the environment conditions. Statement-II: Niche supports numerous species at a time. In the light of above statements, select the correct option given below: [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Given below are two statements with respect to Niche of an organism. Statement-I: A niche is a concept, of how an organism lives or survives in the environment conditions. Statement-II: Niche supports numerous species at a time. In the light of above statements, select the correct option given below: [MHT-CET 2024]
options: A. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
B. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
C. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
D. Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
answer: A. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
solution: Niche supports one species at a time. The niche is specific to each species, which means no two species can share the same niche.

question: Match the types of animals in Column I with their adaptations in Column II and select the correct option. *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Match the types of animals in Column I with their adaptations in Column II and select the correct option. *[MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Eurythermal → Can tolerate a wide range of temperature (II). Stenothermal → Can tolerate only a narrow range of temperature (IV). Euryhaline → Can tolerate a wide range of salinity (III). Stenohaline → Can tolerate only a narrow range of salinity (I).

question: In the equation log S = log C + z log A, that indicates species-area relationship, for smaller areas under study, the value of z (slope of the line) ranges between ______ [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: In the equation $\log S = \log C + z \log A$, that indicates species-area relationship, for smaller areas under study, the value of $z$ (slope of the line) ranges between \underline{\hspace{2cm}} [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: For smaller areas, value of $z$ ranges between 0.1 to 0.2 regardless of species or region under study. But for the larger areas like the entire continents, slopes are closer to vertical axis i.e. steeper.

question: Parasitism is an interaction between two organisms in which ______ [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Parasitism is an interaction between two organisms in which \underline{\hspace{2cm}} [MHT-CET 2023]
options: B. One is harmed and other is benefitted
C. Both are neither harmed nor benefitted
answer: B. One is harmed and other is benefitted
solution: In parasitism, one organism is harmed and the other is benefitted. It is an example of both positive and negative interactions.

question: The given pie chart shows relative share of organisms in the biodiversity. Identify the correct statement. [2022]
question_latex: The given pie chart shows relative share of organisms in the biodiversity. Identify the correct statement. [2022]
options: A. Animal diversity is more than plant diversity.
B. Species diversity of plants and animals is same.
C. Species diversity of fungi and protists is same.
D. Plant diversity is more than animal diversity.
answer: A. Animal diversity is more than plant diversity.
solution: From the pie chart, animal diversity is more than plant diversity.

question: Niche that deals with physical space occupied by the organism is ______ *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Niche that deals with physical space occupied by the organism is \underline{\hspace{2cm}} *[MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Spatial or habitat niche deals with the physical space occupied by the organisms. Trophic niche is on the basis of trophic level of an organism in a food chain.

question: The moving away of an organism temporarily from the stressful habitat to a more hospitable area and return when stressful period is over is called ______ *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: The moving away of an organism temporarily from the stressful habitat to a more hospitable area and return when stressful period is over is called \underline{\hspace{2cm}} *[MHT-CET 2024]
solution: To cope up with extreme variations in their environment, some organisms respond through physiological adjustments, while others do so behaviourally like migration.

question: Hydrophytes are characterised by
question_latex: Hydrophytes are characterised by
solution: The plants which grow in watered areas are called hydrophytes. They are characterised by presence of aerenchyma, poor vascular tissue, poorly developed cuticle, and absence of mechanical tissue.

question: Genetically adapted population to a particular habitat is called
question_latex: Genetically adapted population to a particular habitat is called
solution: An ecotype is a population of individuals of species, which are genetically different. Variations in ecotypes are permanent, irreversible and genetically fixed.

question: Given below are two statements. Statement I: The interaction between two species may be beneficial, harmful or neutral to one of the species or both. Statement II: Competition is a type of interaction where both the species are at loss. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Given below are two statements. Statement I: The interaction between two species may be beneficial, harmful or neutral to one of the species or both. Statement II: Competition is a type of interaction where both the species are at loss. [MHT-CET 2023]
options: A. Statement-I is correct but Statement-II is incorrect.
B. Both statement-I and statement-II are incorrect.
C. Both statement-I and statement-II are correct.
D. Statement-I is incorrect but statement-II is correct.
answer: C. Both statement-I and statement-II are correct.
solution: Both statement-I and statement-II are correct based on interaction of two species.

question: Phenomenal and rapid increase of population in a short period is called
question_latex: Phenomenal and rapid increase of population in a short period is called
solution: The tremendous increase in the size and growth of a population in a short period is known as population explosion.

question: If natality rate is parallel to mortality rate then population [MHT CET 2005]
question_latex: If natality rate is parallel to mortality rate then population [MHT CET 2005]
solution: If natality (i.e. birth rate) is equal to mortality (i.e., death rate) then population will remain stationary.

question: Formation of tropical forests needs mean annual temperature and mean annual precipitation as
question_latex: Formation of tropical forests needs mean annual temperature and mean annual precipitation as
solution: Formation of tropical forests needs mean annual temperature of $18^\circ - 25^\circ$C and mean annual precipitation of $150 - 400$ cm.

question: Competition of species leads to
question_latex: Competition of species leads to
options: C. Greater number of niches are formed
solution: Extinction is the result of competition of species.

question: The number of deaths when environmental pressures come into play is ___ *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: The number of deaths when environmental pressures come into play is \underline{\hspace{2cm}} *[MHT-CET 2024]
solution: The number of deaths when environmental pressures come into play is realized mortality.

question: The desert plants in order to tolerate water stress, show
question_latex: The desert plants in order to tolerate water stress, show
solution: Plants growing in desert are called xerophytes. These have well developed root system, reduced leaves and sunken stomata to reduce transpiration.

question: Position of an organism in the environmental gradient is ______ niche. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Position of an organism in the environmental gradient is \underline{\hspace{2cm}} niche. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Position of an organism in the environmental gradient is hypervolume niche; it considers both biotic and abiotic factors resulting space.

question: Nosema notabilis is an example for
question_latex: \textit{Nosema notabilis} is an example for
solution: A hyperparasite is an organism, which parasitizes on another parasite. Nosema notabilis is a hyperparasite of Spherospora polymorpha, which in turn is a parasite of urinary bladder of toad fish.

question: Match types of population growth rates in Column-I with countries in Column-II and choose the correct option given below. *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Match types of population growth rates in Column-I with countries in Column-II and choose the correct option given below. *[MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Match the columns with population rate and country: Rapid → India (iv), Slow → Australia (i), Zero → Italy (ii), Negative → Germany (iii).

question: The mean annual rainfall of tropical forest ranges between ______ *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: The mean annual rainfall of tropical forest ranges between \underline{\hspace{2cm}} *[MHT-CET 2024]
solution: The mean annual rainfall of tropical forest ranges between 150–400 cm.

question: An unrestricted reproductive capacity is called
question_latex: An unrestricted reproductive capacity is called
solution: Biotic potential is a rate at which a population of a given species will increase when no limits are placed on its rate of growth.

question: Musk deer is an example of [MHT-CET 2019]
question_latex: Musk deer is an example of [MHT-CET 2019]
solution: Musk deer is an example of vulnerable species.

question: A country with a high rate of population growth took measures to reduce it. The figure below shows age sex pyramids of populations A and B twenty years apart. Select the correct interpretation about them.
question_latex: A country with a high rate of population growth took measures to reduce it. The figure below shows age sex pyramids of populations A and B twenty years apart. Select the correct interpretation about them.
options: A. A is more recent and shows slight reduction in the growth rate
B. B is earlier pyramid and shows stabilised growth rate
C. B is more recent showing that population is very young
D. A is the earlier pyramid and no change has occurred in the growth rate
answer: A. A is more recent and shows slight reduction in the growth rate
solution: Interpretation (a) is correct. A is more recent and shows slight reduction in the growth rate.

question: Given below are two statements. Statement-I: Calotropis growing in abandoned fields is never consumed by cattle and goats. Statement-II: Calotropis produces highly poisonous cardiac glycosides. In light of above statements, select the most appropriate answer from the option given below. *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Given below are two statements. Statement-I: Calotropis growing in abandoned fields is never consumed by cattle and goats. Statement-II: Calotropis produces highly poisonous cardiac glycosides. In light of above statements, select the most appropriate answer from the option given below. *[MHT-CET 2024]
options: A. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
B. Statement I is incorrect and statement II is correct.
C. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
D. Statement I is correct and statement II is incorrect.
answer: C. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
solution: Calotropis growing in abandoned fields produces highly poisonous cardiac glycosides and that is why you never see any cattle or goats browsing on this plant. This is an example of chemical means of defense developed by plants.

question: Thick cuticle, sunken stomata, occurrence of CAM pathway and leaves reduced to spines are characteristic features of some plants adapted to ______ conditions. *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Thick cuticle, sunken stomata, occurrence of CAM pathway and leaves reduced to spines are characteristic features of some plants adapted to \underline{\hspace{2cm}} conditions. *[MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Desert adaptations are for minimizing water loss and bearing high intensity of sunlight without causing any harm to the plants.

question: Following figure shows growth curve of population. [2022]
question_latex: Following figure shows growth curve of population. [2022]
solution: The J-shaped growth curve shown in the figure represents exponential growth of population.

question: Based on the graph given below match different biomes in Column I with appropriate annual rainfall in Column II. Choose the correct option. *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Based on the graph given below match different biomes in Column I with appropriate annual rainfall in Column II. Choose the correct option. *[MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Match the columns for biomes and precipitations: Coniferous forest → 50–250 cm (iii), Tropical forest → 150–450 cm (i), Arctic and alpine tundra → Less than 50 cm (iv), Desert → Less than 150 cm (ii).

question: Given below are two statements. Statement I: Stenohaline animals tolerate wide range of salt concentration. Statement II: Euryhaline animals are capable of handling narrow range of salt concentration. In the light of above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Given below are two statements. Statement I: Stenohaline animals tolerate wide range of salt concentration. Statement II: Euryhaline animals are capable of handling narrow range of salt concentration. In the light of above statements, choose the most appropriate answer. [MHT-CET 2023]
options: A. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
B. Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
C. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
D. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
answer: C. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
solution: Both statement I and statement II are incorrect. Stenohaline animals tolerate narrow range of salt concentration. Euryhaline animals are capable of handling wide range of salt concentration.

question: Which spectrum of the light harmful to organism:
question_latex: Which spectrum of the light harmful to organism:
solution: UV component of light is harmful to organisms as it can cause DNA damage and other cellular injuries.

question: Complete the following analogy with respect to habitat of mammals. Fossorial mammals: _A_; Arboreal mammals: B *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Complete the following analogy with respect to habitat of mammals. Fossorial mammals: $A$; Arboreal mammals: $B$ *[MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Fossorial mammals are animals which burrow; example is Kangaroo rats. Arboreal mammals live on trees their whole life; example is Lemurs. So the correct option is B (Kangaroo rats, Lemurs).

question: The sum total of all populations in given habitat is called________ [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: The sum total of all populations in given habitat is called \underline{\hspace{2cm}} [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: Sum total of all populations in a given habitat is called community. Ecosystem is interaction of biotic and abiotic community in given habitat.

question: Density of population increases when___ [MHT-CET 2018]
question_latex: Density of population increases when \underline{\hspace{2cm}} [MHT-CET 2018]
options: C. Mortality increases and immigration decreases
D. Natality decreases and emigration increases
solution: Density of population increases when natality and immigration increases.

question: Which one of the following is INCORRECT regarding Logistic growth curve of population? *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Which one of the following is INCORRECT regarding Logistic growth curve of population? *[MHT-CET 2024]
options: A. It is considered a more realistic one
B. It shows lag phase, followed by phases of acceleration, deceleration and finally an asymptote.
C. It becomes stationary when population density reaches the carrying capacity.
D. It shows that every species is capable of growing exponentially.
answer: D. It shows that every species is capable of growing exponentially.
solution: Logistic growth involves competition between individuals for limited resources which will weed out the weaker ones. Only the fittest individuals will survive and reproduce. So logistic growth does not show exponential growth for every species.

question: Living in same habitat, organisms of same species of form
question_latex: Living in same habitat, organisms of same species form
solution: A population is a group of individuals of a species (same species) growing in a given area (same habitat).

question: Mass of living matter at a trophic level in an area at any time is called
question_latex: Mass of living matter at a trophic level in an area at any time is called
solution: The amount of living matter present in an ecosystem at its different trophic levels is called standing crop. It is expressed in the form of number or biomass measured as either fresh weight or dry weight.

question: Which of the following statements regarding species interdependence are true? I. An Association of two species where one is benefitted and other remains unaffected is called mutualism. II. An interspecific association where both partners derive benefit from each other is called commensalism. III. A direct food relation between two species of animals in which one animal kills and feeds on another is referred as predation. IV. A relationship between two species of organisms where both are partners are benefitted from each other is called symbiosis.
question_latex: Which of the following statements regarding species interdependence are true? I. An Association of two species where one is benefitted and other remains unaffected is called mutualism. II. An interspecific association where both partners derive benefit from each other is called commensalism. III. A direct food relation between two species of animals in which one animal kills and feeds on another is referred as predation. IV. A relationship between two species of organisms where both are partners are benefitted from each other is called symbiosis.
solution: Predation is a direct food relation between two species of animals, in which one animal (the predator) captures and feeds on another (the prey). In symbiosis, two organisms live together in close physical association from which one or both derive benefit. Statements III and IV are correct.

question: Two species occupying same or overlapping area are called as
question_latex: Two species occupying same or overlapping area are called as
solution: Population of two or more species whose geographical ranges or distribution coincide or overlap are known as sympatric species.

question: Keystone species deserve protection because these
question_latex: Keystone species deserve protection because these
options: A. Are capable of surviving in harsh environmental condition
B. Indicate presence of certain minerals in the soil
C. Have become rare due to over exploitation
D. Play an important role in supporting other species
answer: D. Play an important role in supporting other species
solution: Keystone species deserve protection because these have a significant and disproportionately large impact on the other species living in community. Removal or decrease in number of these species causes serious disruption in structure and function of that community.

question: From the given graph of population growth select the correct option having correct value of r and bar graph.
question_latex: From the given graph of population growth select the correct option having correct value of $r$ and bar graph.
solution: A bell-shaped polygon indicates a moderate proportion of young to old. As the rate of growth becomes slow and stable, the pre-reproductive age group become more or less equal in size and post reproductive group remains the smallest. So $r = 0$ with stable pyramid.

question: Large regional terrestrial unit delimited by a specific climatic zone having major vegetation zone and the associated fauna is *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Large regional terrestrial unit delimited by a specific climatic zone having major vegetation zone and the associated fauna is *[MHT-CET 2024]
solution: A biome is a large regional terrestrial unit delimited by a specific climatic zone having major vegetation zone and the associated fauna.

question: According to the most common evolutionary stable strategy (ESS), the ratio of females to males in a population is ______ [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: According to the most common evolutionary stable strategy (ESS), the ratio of females to males in a population is \underline{\hspace{2cm}} [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: According to the most common evolutionary stable strategy (ESS), the ratio of females to males in a population is 1:1 (i.e., 1000:1000).

question: The term 'Niche' denotes the ______ role played by an organism in its environment. *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: The term 'Niche' denotes the \underline{\hspace{2cm}} role played by an organism in its environment. *[MHT-CET 2024]
solution: The term Niche is used to denote the functional role played by an organism in its environment (J. Grinnell 1917).

question: If the strong partner is benefitted and the weak partner is damaged. It is known as
question_latex: If the strong partner is benefitted and the weak partner is damaged. It is known as
solution: Predation is non-symbiotic consertism with damage to one for the benefit of the other. A strong partner kills or damages the weaker one for food.

question: The basic unit of ecological hierarchy is [2022]
question_latex: The basic unit of ecological hierarchy is [2022]
solution: The basic unit of ecological hierarchy is the organism.

question: Organisms of same kind inhabiting a geographical area constitute ___ *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Organisms of same kind inhabiting a geographical area constitute \underline{\hspace{2cm}} *[MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Organisms of same kind inhabiting a geographical area constitute population.

question: Given below are two statements. Statement I: Intraspecific competition occurs between organisms of the same population. Statement II: Such interaction may be beneficial, harmful or neutral to the interacting individuals. In the light of above two statements choose the correct option. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Given below are two statements. Statement I: Intraspecific competition occurs between organisms of the same population. Statement II: Such interaction may be beneficial, harmful or neutral to the interacting individuals. In the light of above two statements choose the correct option. [MHT-CET 2023]
options: A. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
B. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
C. Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
D. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
answer: A. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
solution: Intraspecific competition occurs between organisms of the same population and such interaction may be beneficial, harmful or neutral to the interacting individuals.

question: Populations termed r-strategists [MHT CET 2003]
question_latex: Populations termed $r$-strategists [MHT CET 2003]
solution: During short period of time, r-strategist populations produce many offsprings, which require little care. They have type-III survivorship curves, tend to have J-shaped growth curves, and are generally opportunist species representing pioneer species of new and disturbed habitats.

question: Match the following with reference to ecological organizations. *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Match the following with reference to ecological organizations. *[MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Biome → A large regional terrestrial unit (III). Community → Populations of different species that interact (IV). Population → Organisms of same kind inhabiting an area (II). Organism → Basic unit of ecological hierarchy (I).

question: The nature and properties of soil in different places depend on
question_latex: The nature and properties of soil in different places depend on
solution: The nature and properties of soil in different places depend on climate of the place, transportation of the soil and the weathering process.

question: Which of the following is not true for a species?
question_latex: Which of the following is not true for a species?
options: A. Members of a species can interbreed
B. Variations occur among members of a species
C. Each species is reproductively isolated from every other species
D. Gene flow does not occur between the populations of a species
answer: D. Gene flow does not occur between the populations of a species
solution: Gene flow means the spread of genes through population as affected by movements of individuals and their propagules, e.g., spores, seeds etc. Gene flow ensures that all populations of a given species share a common gene pool, i.e., it reduces difference between populations. So gene flow does occur between populations of a species.

question: Find odd one out, according to parasitism. [MHT CET 2009]
question_latex: Find odd one out, according to parasitism. [MHT CET 2009]
solution: Plasmodium is odd one as it is a digenetic endoparasite with man as the primary host and female Anopheles mosquito as the vector, while lice, bedbug and mites all are blood sucking ectoparasites.

question: Which one of the following food chain starts with dead decaying organic matter? [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: Which one of the following food chain starts with dead decaying organic matter? [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: Detritus food chain starts from dead and decaying organic matter. Grazing, aquatic and desert food chains start from producers as a source of food.

question: Density of population fluctuates during a given period due to changes in four basic processes namely birth, immigration (I) and emigration (E). If 'N' is the population density at time 't', then its density at time 't+1' can be calculated as. *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Density of population fluctuates during a given period due to changes in four basic processes namely birth, immigration ($I$) and emigration ($E$). If $N$ is the population density at time $t$, then its density at time $t+1$ can be calculated as. *[MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Density of population in a habitat during a given period fluctuates due to changes in four basic processes. Deaths (D) and Emigration (E) lead to decrease in population density. Birth (B) and Immigration (I) increase the population density. Therefore: $N_{t+1} = N_t + (B+I) - (D+E)$

question: The interaction of species with the environment is called as
question_latex: The interaction of species with the environment is called as
solution: Autecology deals with the interaction of species with environment.

question: Which one of the following conditions represent zero population growth? [2022]
question_latex: Which one of the following conditions represent zero population growth? [2022]
solution: Zero population growth occurs when birth rate equals death rate.

question: Most of the lichens are composed of [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: Most of the lichens are composed of [MHT-CET 2020]
options: D. Blue green algae and Phycomycetes
solution: Most of the lichens are formed from green algae (Chlorophyceae) and Ascomycetes sac fungi.

question: A population growing in a habitat with limited resources shows four phases of growth in the following sequence
question_latex: A population growing in a habitat with limited resources shows four phases of growth in the following sequence
solution: When a population is growing in limited resource, the population growth consists of five phases: (i) Lag phase: No or very little growth. (ii) Accelerating phase: Growth in the beginning. (iii) Exponential phase: Number of individuals increases at a logarithmic rate. (iv) Deceleration phase: Rate of population increase slows down. (v) Stationary phase: Essentially no net change.

question: The organisms inhabiting a common environment belong to the same
question_latex: The organisms inhabiting a common environment belong to the same
solution: A community is any assemblage of populations of living organisms in a prescribed area of habitat. All the organisms of a community live together, share same habitat and influence each other's life directly or indirectly.

question: The presence of claws and adhesive pads on the legs of cockroach is an example of _____ adaptation. [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: The presence of claws and adhesive pads on the legs of cockroach is an example of \underline{\hspace{2cm}} adaptation. [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: Cursorial adaptations are adaptations for running for which presence of claws and adhesive pads are found in legs of cockroaches.

question: Given below are two statements. Statement I: Habitat consists of numerous niches. Statement II: Niche is an area where species lives and prospers. In the light of above statements choose the correct option. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Given below are two statements. Statement I: Habitat consists of numerous niches. Statement II: Niche is an area where species lives and prospers. In the light of above statements choose the correct option. [MHT-CET 2023]
options: A. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
B. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
C. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
D. Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
answer: A. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
solution: Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect. Niche is the functional role played by an organism in its environment, not just an area where species lives.

question: What type of human population is represented by the following age pyramid?
question_latex: What type of human population is represented by the following age pyramid?
solution: An age pyramid with post-reproductive group larger than pre-reproductive group (urn-shaped) represents a declining population.

question: Match the following in Column I with the means adopted to tide over periods of unfavorable conditions in Column II. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Match the following in Column I with the means adopted to tide over periods of unfavorable conditions in Column II. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Birds → Migration (iii). Seeds → Dormancy (i). Snails → Aestivation (iv). Polar bear → Hibernation (ii).

question: Halophytes are
question_latex: Halophytes are
solution: Halophytes are plants growing in saline soil, i.e., these plants are salt resistant.

question: Organisms possessing strong and stout forelimbs with clawed digits show [MHT-CET 2017]
question_latex: Organisms possessing strong and stout forelimbs with clawed digits show [MHT-CET 2017]
solution: Organisms possessing strong and stout forelimbs with clawed digits show fossorial (burrowing) adaptations.

question: The number of births when environmental pressures come into play is *[MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: The number of births when environmental pressures come into play is *[MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Absolute Natality is the number of births under ideal conditions (with no competition, abundance of resources such as food and water, etc.). Realized Natality is the number of births when environmental pressures come into play.

question: Which of the following are predators of land plants? [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Which of the following are predators of land plants? [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Cattle are predators of land plants as they are herbivorous.

question: Good soil is that which
question_latex: Good soil is that which
options: A. Holds whole of the water that enters into it
B. Allows percolating the water slowly from it
C. Allows water to pass very quickly from it
D. Allows limited amount of water to retain into it
answer: B. Allows percolating the water slowly from it
solution: The good soil is that which allows percolating the water slowly from it, e.g., alluvial soil (soil carried by water).

question: Secondary metabolites like nicotine, opium etc. are produced by certain plants ______ [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Secondary metabolites like nicotine, opium etc. are produced by certain plants \underline{\hspace{2cm}} [MHT-CET 2023]
options: C. for removal of unwanted toxins
D. as defence against herbivorous animals
answer: D. as defence against herbivorous animals
solution: Secondary metabolites like nicotine, opium etc. are produced by certain plants as defense against herbivorous animals, grazers and browsers.

question: In ecological interaction, an orchid growing as an epiphyte on a branch of mango tree is an example of ........[2022]
question_latex: In ecological interaction, an orchid growing as an epiphyte on a branch of mango tree is an example of ........[2022]
solution: An orchid growing as an epiphyte on a branch of mango tree is an example of commensalism, where the orchid benefits by getting support and sunlight while the mango tree is unaffected.
